authority_ceilings: no live provider calls authorized (offline byte census only; zero pre-gate provider contact)
no live responses or response collection authorized
no private key content or key exposure authorized
pre-exposure gate remains unsigned by this ruling
bounded_unknowns: Reasoning/thinking tokens on gpt-5.6-luna and kimi-k3 are not bounded by the 768 max_output cap (emission/billing treatment unknown pre-gate); claude-haiku-4.5 thinking bills as output tokens and IS bounded by 768.
Cached-input treatment: the worst case assumes NO caching benefit (no discount applied); OpenRouter-route cache passthrough is pending exact confirmation.

Provider-side chat-envelope / role / BOS / special-token overhead beyond the counted payload bytes is not in the byte census; partially absorbed by the 1.0 tok/byte paranoid bound + the 1.05 allowance but not guaranteed.
Slot->route mapping (1=haiku,2=luna,3=kimi) is an assumption confirmed at preflight; the adversarial-mapping arm bounds the ceiling under ANY permutation.

Item bank is state=candidate (item_bank_freeze_authorized=false); the census is valid for the fixed-candidate schedule as committed and MUST be recomputed if the freeze mutates any component; bank component identity re-checked at preflight.
The exact per-provider tokenizer token census is deferred to emission actuals; it is disclosed as pending, not inferred from bytes.

ceiling_recomputation.slot_balance: byte-identical across the three subject slots; total invariant to any slot->route permutation
ceiling_recomputation.worst_case_basis: paranoid 1.0 tok/byte over-count x adversarial all-metered-on-luna mapping; includes the 344-attempt output ceiling (264192 tokens) and the 1.05 input allowance

census_method.disclosure: the 'exact census' pending closes at emission actuals (provider usage fields), disclosed as such; the ceiling is checked against disclosed conservative over-count bounds, not inferred provider counts

rationale: Owner ruling R6-closure (AskUserQuestion interview, Claude Code session, 2026-07-17, America/Sao_Paulo) closes the two pending markers carried by SCORING-R6-ADOPTED-v1: the $8.00-ceiling prompt census method and the subject-provider-model routes / price-table rows. Interview A authorized an OFFLINE byte census plus a disclosed conservative bytes->tokens bound (zero pre-gate provider contact), with the exact per-provider tokenizer census deferred to emission usage actuals and disclosed as pending. Interview B amended the routes: claude-haiku-4.5 and gpt-5.6-luna move to the OpenRouter route (provider-preference upstream pinning recorded in the emission config); kimi-k3 stays on the Moonshot kimi.com coding-plan subscription route at $0.00 marginal, subscription-route disclosure mandatory. Price rows are pinned from sources fetched live 2026-07-17 (OpenRouter listing corroborated by the Anthropic pricing table for haiku; OpenRouter listing for luna; kimi $0 marginal subscription). The offline census computes a global worst case of USD 2.246177, UNDER the USD 8.00 ceiling at 28.1 percent. No provider call, no key exposure, no merge is authorized, and the pre-exposure gate remains unsigned.
